text: Hello students, Welcome to GITsmeasures In today's video I am going to explain HDFS that is Hadoop Distributed File System How does its whole architecture works characteristics, all the points I will explain you in this video with example So guys quickly like the video Subscribe to the channel If you haven't done it yet, if you have done it Then you can make a subscriber from the device Subscribers are very important So let's start First of all, what is the purpose of HDFS that is Hadoop Distributed File System? What is its main role? Its role is that we talk about big data That we have a huge amount of data How to store a large amount of data? The way to store it is HDFS tells us Because whenever we talk about data processing So two things are very important Two big challenges come The first challenge comes How to store data? Because we have a huge amount of data So storing it is a challenge in itself Now when stored So how to process it? So that we can get some important information from it So the processing part is seen by MapReduce And the storage part is how to store efficiently So that is actually HDFS So it is a very robust file system And it is based on your traditional file system Means the file system we normally use Like we use in Windows and Linux We use hierarchical file system In which we make directories We make files in directories The same hierarchical architecture is used in HDFS But you can say that it is designed Like we use NTFS in Windows Or use NFS What is it actually? Small data is designed for a limited amount of data But it is designed for a huge amount of data Now let's talk about its structure first So in the structure of your HDFS What is this HDFS client? You can say that it is a client Who has to put data Who has to extract data All those things Who will read the data Who will write the data But if we talk about the structure of HDFS So here two main components come The first component comes Name node Name node Load it It is a name node You can also call it a boss You can also call it a master node Okay This is the boss here That is a name node And then we come to the employees Like there are employees in companies Who work under the boss And what is here? Data nodes Where data is actually stored Like we say that I have Let's say I have some data I have to store that data In it, you can say any file There is text data, images, videos The actual data that will be stored It will be here It will be on these servers But the data that will be stored How to do it Which data will go where All those things are your name node And a very important thing here That many people think Sir, because it is a new thing So maybe we need new devices We have to buy new hardware New devices No This is also the biggest thing about HDFS That the commodity The already existing hardware It also works properly on them You don't need to buy new devices So it is also cost effective Okay So here Let's say a file comes Let's say I have a file 520 MB file Okay Now what will it do to the file of 520 MB It will not store it directly Let's divide it Like we normally divide the file Let's store it Whether you talk about Windows in your laptop Let's talk about Linux There too we divide the file If I have a huge amount of file So what will we do to it Let's not store it directly Let's divide it Okay By partitioning Here we have a partition By default 128 MB In our normal systems Partition is 4 KB 8 KB Because we talk about limited amount of data in it Here is a huge amount of data So the partition of the file in itself The smallest block Which you can call What will be the size of a block 128 MB So let's say if I have a file of 520 MB So you can say 128 MB is one 128 MB is second 128 MB is third 128 MB is fourth Okay So these four How many will be 512 MB How many are left later Later your 8 more are left So 8 MB will come in the next one So that means The first 128 MB block will come I will store it in this data node That's why you are saying distributed HDFS is distributed Not in one place You can store all 520 here But don't store here It is a distributed So that I can access it parallelly during processing If I store it in one place Then the whole burden will come on it So 128 MB here 128 MB here 128 MB here 128 MB here And the last 8 MB is here So in this way Distributed Stores data in a distributed way Now how will you know The user said that I have to read the file I have to read a particular block So how do I know I kept it here I kept it here Where did I keep it So it has metadata Every file has a metadata Means the information related to that file What is the file name What are the permissions on the file Which data node did you keep How many replications of it were made Means the information related to that data It is stored in the name node So what happens here File system name space Name space Where all the metadata related to the file Which is stored Name of the file Size Permissions How many replications you made How many you kept Which place where you kept All those things are stored here So this is how the structure actually works Now next here The concept of replication comes Means we are here in HDFS Let's say I have a block of 128 So I stored that block here I kept its name here Let's say I stored it here Is my work over No HDFS says I work on the replication factor Replication means That I will not store this whole block In one place Its same copy Will be stored somewhere else Means the data is the same I copied the same data to three places I picked up the second block I stored it here too I stored it here too I stored it here too So by default Replication factor is 3 Means one block is stored in three places What did it get It got fault tolerance Fault tolerance means If one of the data node is down Then how will I get data The client will keep waiting When will I get data No We need to get data accessed 24x7 fast Need speed So I'm there Okay it's down So get the data from here Now how did you know There is a store here and here How did you know My data was here too And the same data was here Name node will tell So this is the story of name node That's why we are calling it boss We are calling it master Okay So this is your replication So that I get fault tolerance So this is how it works Then One more concept comes in this Rack awareness Rack awareness Now what is the concept of rack awareness Now we don't have one or two data nodes We have thousands of data nodes Because we are storing a very large amount of data Like Google, Facebook They have limited store They have a lot of store So what they have to do Like I have these three nodes So I made a rack of them Okay Like here you will have more clarity from this diagram Rack means Your rack is like this Your rack is like this Like this is a rack Like we keep rack in the house So that rack I put the first block in this rack Now I will not keep the copy of it again in this I will keep its copy in some other rack So that the same type of data does not come in one rack Because if there is a problem in the rack Then all the data will be lost Will not be able to access all the data So I put one block here I put one block here Put the third of the second block here Put the next here Now the second copy of one They will put it in some other rack And the third copy is in some other remote area And what do we do here The one copy is that we present the data locally What does local mean Rather than That I have to access the data again and again I go to the remote place and access No, I keep that data locally And whenever the user says that I want data So do you know what HDFS will do He will give the address of that nearest rack Or the nearest data node Will give the IP address So that you do not have to travel too much Data does not have to travel too much See, it is a simple example Let's say I live in Chandigarh I booked a Royal Enfield bike Now what will happen here I booked a bike Now where is the bike being made Royal Enfield bike actually what is made Made in Tamil Nadu Made near Chennai Now my order will go there From there the bike will be transported Chandigarh will come here Then I will get It will take a lot of time So what did Royal Enfield do Made it there But they have made outlets in Chandigarh, Mumbai, Delhi You go to Chandigarh You booked there You got it immediately So you have kept the data locally Rather than it remotely Yes, the copies are kept remotely So that if there is a problem in the local Then we have a place in the remote So this is actually the story It works like this Then we have the function of NameNode Now we have two things in NameNode One is FS image And one is EditLog What is FS image Which is my entire file system You can say its image Now this is the entire file system Its image Image means which file is in which file Which directory Size An image of all things is made And it actually stores a file here You can say it as a primary file Along with this, there is an EditLog Now what is this EditLog You deleted the file So it will become a log That yes you deleted a file Changed the location Changed the path All those things will be edited by you At this place So it actually works like this And along with that Let me tell you one more point here That another functionality works here That is Secondary NameNode Secondary NameNode Secondary NameNode is also here Like NameNode and DataNode There is another third component Secondary NameNode What is Secondary NameNode Actually It is its PA That is the boss It has a PA Whatever changes you are doing It keeps on doing those changes It keeps on updating the file image Means this FS file This file FS It will keep on updating this file Because NameNode is busy NameNode is busy So as soon as it gets time It will save this updated file in NameNode So what is Secondary NameNode It is a PA Which is scheduling all the meetings Keeping all the changes And what is this Whatever changes I have done All those changes It has stored here By taking all those changes It updated the FS image file And by updating that file It put it in NameNode So that NameNode is completely updated But who does this work Secondary NameNode Because it is its PA You can call it its Assistant Helper So in this way The whole story runs And here a heartbeat message It has another responsibility Checking the heartbeat message Means DataNode After every 3 seconds It sends a heartbeat message to NameNode So that NameNode knows That my DataNode is running properly It is live If after 3 seconds No message has come from any DataNode So it is its replication It runs So that data is available So this is how Because it is distributed Data is not lying in one place Everything is distributed So I told you the functionality of DataNode Now here When we have to read and write So read and write Obviously you know How will all of them work Read and write If I want to read data How will we read the data The client said I want to read a particular data So on NameNode First it will get permission Can it read? If it can read So where have we stored that data So by checking with metadata We will find out where it is stored Let's say it is stored here So its address Will give it to the client The client will not access the DataNode directly Will access through NameNode And then we will read the data It has There is a little problem in the case of write You are doing write once But you can read many times There is no problem What is the problem in write That you are changing in data Now see The client said I have to change in data Let's say the value of x was 100 It has to be changed to 200 Now do you know what is the problem It will be here too Its copy is lying here It will be here too And its copy is lying here It will be here too So write You have to do it all in parallel So in this case Write You can say a little expensive Because not at once You have to do it all at once Parallel simultaneously Have to update So this is how Read and write Is actually performed in HDFS So this is full information about HDFS Thank you
